description: Wire all orphaned mk_brain modules into the unified mk_entry.cpp system. Connect learning_engine, brain_memory, embeddings_eng, ann_search, cache_mgr, daily_briefing, fact_extractor/biographical, and reasoning_engine into the MKSystem struct so they are initialized at boot and available to the REPL.
steps: Add #include directives in mk_entry.cpp for all the unwired mk_brain modules: ../mk_brain/learning/learning_engine.cpp, ../mk_brain/memory/brain_memory.cpp, ../mk_brain/embeddings/embeddings_eng.cpp, ../mk_brain/vector_search/ann_search.cpp, ../mk_brain/cashe/cache_mgr.cpp, ../mk_brain/daily_briefing.cpp, ../mk_brain/fact_extractor/biographical.cpp, ../mk_brain/reasoning/reasoning_engine.cpp. Place them after the existing mk_brain/personality/response_style.cpp include.

Add corresponding member instances to the MKSystem struct: MKLearningEngine learningEngine; MKBrainMemory brainMemory; MKANNSearch vectorSearch; MKCacheManager cacheManager; MKDailyBriefing dailyBriefing; MKBiographicalExtractor factExtractor; MKEmbeddingsEngine embeddings; MKReasoningEngine reasoningEngine;
In the MKSystem constructor, initialize them with sensible defaults. Call learningEngine.restore() to load persisted knowledge. The ANN search can use default 128 dims.
Wire the learning engine into the /learn command: after sys.graph.persistNewFact(), also call sys.learningEngine.learnFact(source, relation, target) so facts go into both systems.

Wire the biographical fact extractor into handle_natural_query(): after processing a natural language query, call sys.factExtractor.extractFromMessage(input) to passively learn biographical facts from conversation.

Wire the brain memory (dialog context) into the REPL: call sys.brainMemory.commitToShortTerm("user", input) for each user message and sys.brainMemory.commitToShortTerm("mk", response) for each MK response.
Wire the vector search into the GRAPH route as a fallback: when graph.getAll() returns empty, try vectorSearch.search(input) and if results are found with score > 0.3, use them as context.
Wire the daily briefing: add a check at boot (after sys.graph.loadAllKnowledge()) that if sys.dailyBriefing.shouldGenerate() is true, generate and print a briefing with mock system data.
Add a /briefing command to the REPL that generates and displays the daily briefing on demand.
In the graceful shutdown section, call sys.learningEngine.persist() to save learned facts.
Verify the build compiles with all new includes.
acceptance_criteria: MKSystem struct has instances of all mk_brain module classes
The /learn command stores facts in both the pattern graph AND the learning engine
Natural language queries passively trigger biographical fact extraction
Dialog context is tracked via brainMemory for each turn
The /briefing command works in the REPL
Vector search is used as a fallback in the GRAPH route
